posts.text: Don’t ask whose fault this was. Plenty of time for second-guessing and recriminations. Ask instead, what can you do? For my part, that means telling the truth as I see it, as long as I can. The media will be under a lot of pressure to toe the line; don’t capitulate in advance
posts.quote: Don’t ask whose fault this was. Plenty of time for second-guessing and recriminations. Ask instead, what can you do? For my part, that means telling the truth as I see it, as long as I can. The media will be under a lot of pressure to toe the line; don’t capitulate in advance

posts.text: If this goes the way it seems to be going, a plea: Hold the recriminations. The important thing will be figuring out how to fight the climate of repression, which will begin via preemptive obedience long before the government formally changes hands
posts.quote: If this goes the way it seems to be going, a plea: Hold the recriminations. The important thing will be figuring out how to fight the climate of repression, which will begin via preemptive obedience long before the government formally changes hands

posts.text: More on Harris closing the economy gap: partisan positions, especially R, largely immovable. So looking at improvement btwn Sept and Oct Times polls, it looks as if Harris had huge success in persuading the persuadable

posts.text: Some worry that America may become a soft autocracy like Hungary, with one-party rule but not violent repression. We should be so lucky. nytimes.com/2024/11/04/opini…
posts.quote: Some worry that America may become a soft autocracy like Hungary, with one-party rule but not violent repression. We should be so lucky. nytimes.com/2024/11/04/opini…

posts.text: One possibly important factor in the election: People are still a bit mad about high prices, but historical patterns suggest that most of this effect should have faded by now briefingbook.info/p/digestin…
posts.quote: One possibly important factor in the election: People are still a bit mad about high prices, but historical patterns suggest that most of this effect should have faded by now briefingbook.info/p/digestin…

posts.text: I wonder how much Trump being the boy who cried “depression” has helped neutralize his once formidable advantage on the economy
posts.quote: I wonder how much Trump being the boy who cried “depression” has helped neutralize his once formidable advantage on the economy

posts.text: Incredibly, people are still writing stories about how only the elite has benefited from the strong economy. In fact, the biggest gains have gone to low-wage workers
posts.quote: Incredibly, people are still writing stories about how only the elite has benefited from the strong economy. In fact, the biggest gains have gone to low-wage workers

posts.text: More on the incredible shrinking economy issue: at this point it looks like an election over abortion rights and immigration. And that Selzer poll suggests that we may be underestimating the POW — pissed-off women — vote

posts.text: Polling suggests that Harris has more or less erased Trump’s advantage on the economy. Much of that is probably the reality of falling inflation. But a well-focused campaign has surely helped. nytimes.com/2024/11/04/us/po…
posts.quote: Polling suggests that Harris has more or less erased Trump’s advantage on the economy. Much of that is probably the reality of falling inflation. But a well-focused campaign has surely helped. nytimes.com/2024/11/04/us/po…

posts.text: Trump says that armed migrants have taken over our cities. If so, they’re doing a remarkably good job of maintaining order nytimes.com/2024/11/02/upsho…

posts.text:

What has happened since the last time he predicted a depression if a Democrat won:

posts.text: Hi all, Paul Krugman and I did a podcast on the Trump Administration and what might be happening in the bombing of Iran. Not the most uplifting of talks, but you might find it interesting. substack.com/@phillipspob...

posts.text: Trump’s declaration that he expects UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER from Iran does not seem to be calming markets

posts.text: excellent deep dive into all of the ways that the Trump administration has used the federal budget to keep punishing critics and trying to bend them to his will.    FWIW, this is precisely how Viktor Orban crushed the opposition in Hungary 15 years ago.  The national budget is a powerful weapon.

posts.text: Many people talking about how gasoline prices at the pump have already jumped. But what should really terrify Republicans is RBOB — the futures price on wholesale gasoline. This is up 75 cents a gallon since its low earlier this year.
